# My super cool website that analyzes the effectiveness of LS assingments on understanding in comp 110

This webstie uses a bar graph, a box plot, a cat plot and a scatter plot to visulize studetent responses. The bar graph shows how many students generally thought the LS assignments were helpful. The scatter plot, though proved to not be helpful, attempted to analyze the relationship between LS effectiveness and student understanding. The cat plot did a better job at this analysis and was used to determine the upwards trend between students who have higher lsqs_effectiveness and those who have a higher understanding. Finally, since the scatter plot was not effective, we included a box plot that utalizes error bars to futher analyze how lsqa_effectiveness relates to understanding.

## Conclusion

Given the analysis we performed, our data supports the idea that LS questions aid with understaning of course material. Our histogram portrays that the majority of students rated LS questions as effective. Further, our catplot demonstrates a positive correlation between LS questions effectiveness and understanding, with an effectiveness rating of 6 being associated with the greatest udnerstanding rating. While some of our analysis shows that low LS effectiveness is also associated with high udenrstanding, this may occur since some students have previous programming expereince, and therefore have a high udnerstanding of the material without finding the LS questions effective. Given our analysis, we recommend that the the course includes a greater number of LS question assignments because they improve udnerstanding of material, which will in turn improve overall grades for students. One refinement to this idea is that there should be more LS assignments depending on the topic. For example, harder topics like recursion could have a greater number of LS question assignments. A cost of this idea is that the isntructional team will have to spend more time creating these assignments. Further, it is a larger number of assignemnts for the instructional team to grade. Despite this, the overall student beenfit of greater understanding of course material outweights the costs.
